description: On-platform GetAgent backtests for every Playbook the factory authored. A systematic 2x3 study (two strategies x BTC/ETH/SOL) plus an ETH mean-reversion. The four BTC/ETH winners are published; the three losers are withheld. Metrics are as returned by the Bitget GetAgent sandbox (real equity curves); 'budget_return_pct' is net PnL over the $1,000 strategy margin budget, 'account_net_usd' is the change on the 100,000 USDT venue balance.

published.name: adaptive-regime-eth
published.note: Best risk-adjusted ratios of the set, but a near-flat absolute return — the conflict-gate stayed highly selective on ETH. Published as a genuine positive; not a strong absolute performer.

withheld.name: mean-reversion-eth
withheld.reason: Fading a sustained ETH trend lost money — exactly the failure mode the strategy's own docs warn about.

withheld.name: momentum-breakout-sol
withheld.reason: Breakout edge that holds on BTC/ETH did not hold on SOL (breakeven-to-negative); SOL's volatility produced more false breakouts.

withheld.name: adaptive-regime-sol
withheld.reason: Regime edge also broke on SOL (PF < 1, negative Sharpe). Both strategies fail on SOL — a clean asset-specific result.

summary.finding: Both strategies win on BTC and ETH and break on SOL's higher volatility. The factory publishes only what the evidence earns.
summary.reproduce: Upload each package in playbook/<name>/ via POST /api/v1/playbook/upload, then POST /api/v1/playbook/run with the returned draft_id; poll GET /api/v1/playbook/run?run_id=...
